relationship_to_restored_text: Interpretive contemplative layer aligned by verse. This does not replace the source-audited restored translation.

verses.mystical_translation: The freed people enter wilderness one month after departure.
Hunger turns the whole congregation into grumbling.
Fear remembers Egypt as full pots and forgets bondage.
YHWH rains daily bread to train trust.
The sixth day prepares double for rest.
Evening will teach who brought them out.
Morning glory will answer grumbling.
Bread and flesh are given so the people know YHWH hears.
The grumbling people are invited to draw near.
Glory appears in cloud toward wilderness.
YHWH speaks provision.
Evening flesh and morning bread reveal YHWH.
Quail covers camp; dew covers ground.
The dew lifts and a fine mystery remains.
They ask, What is it? and receive bread from YHWH.
Each gathers according to need.
Some gather more and some less.
Measured by omer, no one has excess and no one lacks.
Daily bread cannot be hoarded.
Hoarding decays.
Morning by morning, need is met before heat.
The sixth day brings double.
Sabbath rest is announced with prepared food.
What is kept for Sabbath does not decay.
Today is Sabbath; eat what has been given.
Six days gather, seventh day rest.
Some still search for what rest says will not be there.
YHWH asks how long refusal will continue.
Sabbath is gift, not lack.
The people rest on the seventh day.
Manna tastes like honeyed wonder.
An omer is kept so generations can see wilderness bread.
A jar becomes memory vessel.
Manna is kept before the testimony.
Forty years the people eat wilderness bread.
The measure is remembered.

contemplative_note: Exodus 16 is daily trust: bread that cannot be hoarded, enoughness without excess or lack, and Sabbath as the first rhythm of freed bodies.
